title: althaea officinalis subsp. taurinensis
intro: Althaea officinalis subsp. taurinensis, commonly known as marshmallow, is a perennial plant in the mallow family (Malvaceae) native to Europe and western Asia, characterized by its large, round leaves and small, pink or purple flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which are attributed to its mucilaginous sap. The health benefits of Althaea officinalis subsp. taurinensis include relief from digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Althaea officinalis subsp. taurinensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
